mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within two weeks.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in 65% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation confirms healthy economic output without price pressures, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually, or the mechanism fails to generate positive alpha in low-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching 3.0 percent forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in 70% of high-inflation observations, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in 60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP confirm recessionary conditions that suppress corporate revenues, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 50% of recession-confirmed quarters, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when monetary policy aggressively cuts rates.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in 65% of positive-INDPRO-momentum periods.
